header.title: Company Formation Services
header.subtitle: Expert UK company incorporation and establishment. From limited companies to LLPs, we handle everything under the Companies Act 2006.

overview.title: Establishing Your UK Business Foundation
paragraphs: Forming a UK company is the cornerstone of establishing a serious, credible business entity. Whether you're launching a startup, setting up a subsidiary, or consolidating existing operations, choosing the right legal structure matters. Our company formation service combines comprehensive legislative knowledge with hands-on support to ensure your business is established correctly from day one.

We specialise in navigating the Companies House registration process, managing articles of association, establishing company governance, and ensuring you meet all statutory obligations from incorporation onwards. Our team has processed hundreds of incorporations, from straightforward single-director companies to complex multi-shareholder corporate structures.

Every company we form is registered with Companies House under the Companies Act 2006 and comes with proper documentation, statutory registers, and a clear governance framework tailored to your business needs.

services.title: UK Company Incorporations (Private Limited Companies)
paragraphs: A private limited company (Ltd) is the most popular business structure in the UK, offering liability protection and tax efficiency. When you incorporate with Axsuma, we handle every detail: from submitting your incorporation documents to Companies House through to delivering your completed incorporation package.

The incorporation process begins with choosing a unique company name that complies with Companies House naming rules and is not already registered. We verify name availability, ensure compliance with the Sensitive Words List, and confirm trade mark clearance. Your memorandum and articles of association are prepared bespoke to your structure, outlining shareholdings, director powers, shareholder rights, voting procedures, and dividend distributions in line with the Companies Act 2006.

We prepare all required statutory documents including the application for incorporation (Form IN01), confirmation of the company secretary (if applicable), statement of initial officers, and statement of capital and initial shareholdings. All documents are digitally signed and submitted to Companies House, with submission typically completed within 1-2 working days. You'll receive your Certificate of Incorporation, company registration number (CRN), and a complete corporate governance package ready for operations.

Our incorporations include setup of statutory registers (members, directors, secretaries, charges, and persons with significant control), issuance of share certificates, and registration for corporation tax with HMRC. We ensure your company is fully tax-registered and ready for trading immediately upon incorporation.

services.title: Ready-Made Companies (Off-the-Shelf)
paragraphs: For businesses that need to operate immediately, ready-made companies offer instant trading capability. We maintain a stock of pre-incorporated companies with unique names, registered shares, and clean statutory registers. You acquire the company through a transfer of shares, change directors and shareholders through standard resolutions, and commence trading the same day.

Ready-made companies are ideal for fast-moving ventures, time-sensitive acquisitions, or situations where waiting for Companies House processing is commercially unviable. The company arrives fully registered with Companies House, with a current Confirmation Statement, and registered with HMRC for corporation tax and PAYE. You simply provide your director, shareholder, and registered office details, we update the registers, and you're live.

Each ready-made company includes a clean cap table, blank statutory registers, and a resignation letter from the existing director (who is Axsuma's nominee). The transfer process is completed within hours, making ready-made companies perfect for time-critical mandates such as immediate trading requirements, acquisition closings, or regulatory submissions under tight deadlines.

We maintain a diverse range of company names across multiple industries and sectors. All ready-made companies comply fully with the Companies Act 2006 and Companies House requirements, with no hidden liabilities or historical baggage. You're acquiring a clean, dormant company structure ready for your business operations.

services.title: Registered Office & Address Services
paragraphs: Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs) combine the flexibility of partnership with corporate liability protection. Regulated by the Limited Liability Partnerships Act 2000 and the Limited Liability Partnerships Regulations 2001, LLPs are particularly popular in professional services, accounting, law, and management consulting. Unlike traditional partnerships, LLP members (equivalent to directors) enjoy limited liability protection—you're not personally liable for the negligence or misconduct of other members.

LLP formation mirrors company incorporation but with distinct statutory requirements. We prepare the incorporation application (Form LLP IN01) alongside the LLP agreement, which defines member contributions, profit-sharing arrangements, management rights, member exit procedures, and dispute resolution. Unlike companies with prescriptive articles, LLP agreements offer flexibility and are contractually binding on all members. Members also have designated membership interests rather than shares, with taxation typically passing through to individual members rather than at the LLP level.

The incorporation process involves submitting incorporation documents to Companies House, registration of initial members, and filing the LLP agreement. Upon completion, you'll receive confirmation of the LLP's registration number and incorporation certificate. We then register the LLP with HMRC for corporation tax and any relevant regulatory bodies (such as the Solicitors Regulation Authority for legal LLPs or the Institute of Chartered Accountants for accounting partnerships).

LLPs must file annual accounts and confirmation statements with Companies House, though they benefit from certain filing exemptions available to smaller LLPs. We ensure your LLP is fully established, properly governed, and registered with all relevant authorities from day one.

Every company incorporated in the UK must have a registered office address where statutory notices, documents, and correspondence can be delivered. This is a mandatory Companies House filing requirement under the Companies Act 2006, and the registered office address is publicly searchable on the Companies House register. Many growing businesses use our professional registered office service rather than listing their home or temporary office address.

Our central London registered office services provide a prestigious business address that enhances your company's corporate profile. We receive all statutory and legal correspondence on your behalf, including documents from Companies House, HMRC, and regulatory bodies. Our service includes secure mail handling, scanning of important documents for your online portal, and weekly email alerts of any correspondence received. We maintain confidentiality and professionalism, never disclosing your details beyond what's required by law.

The registered office is distinct from your trading address or operational premises. Many companies operating from home offices, flexible workspaces, or multiple locations use a professional registered office to present a permanent, credible corporate presence. Directors' home addresses remain private (filed with Companies House under private register provisions) while the registered office appears on public filings.

Should your circumstances change—relocation, office closure, or business restructuring—you can update your registered office through a simple Companies House Form CH01, which we'll prepare and file on your behalf. Our registered office service includes the option to maintain this address indefinitely as your company evolves, with no additional setup costs when you incorporate or register a new entity with us.

why_cards.title: Companies Act 2006 Expertise
why_cards.description: Deep knowledge of UK company law, statutory obligations, and best-practice governance. Every incorporation reflects current legislative requirements and future compliance needs.

why_cards.title: Speed and Efficiency
why_cards.description: Companies House submissions completed within 24-48 hours. Ready-made companies available for same-day trading. No delays, no bureaucracy—just clean, fast incorporation.

why_cards.title: Comprehensive Governance
why_cards.description: Articles tailored to your structure. Statutory registers properly maintained. Director protections and shareholder rights clearly defined. Compliance built in from day one.

cta.title: Ready to Incorporate Your UK Company?
cta.body: Discuss your business structure, incorporation timeline, and governance needs. We'll recommend the ideal formation approach for your situation.

header.title: Company Secretarial & Governance Services
header.subtitle: We support organisations in establishing and operating robust, proportionate governance frameworks that stand up to regulatory scrutiny and scale with growth.

overview.title: Practical Governance, Delivered with Precision
paragraphs: The role of company secretaries, in-house legal teams, and compliance teams is becoming increasingly complex. We support our clients as an extension of their team, whenever and however they need us. This could be interim support during busy periods, specialist project assistance, a fully outsourced company secretarial function, all tailored support to suit your specific needs.

Our company secretarial services ensure your company remains compliant with the Companies Act 2006, Companies House filing obligations, and corporate governance best practices. Our team manages the complete framework of statutory obligations, including maintaining registers, filing annual confirmations, managing director changes, handling persons with significant control (PSC) notifications, and keeping corporate records current and compliant.

We can be formally appointed as your corporate Company Secretary or provide flexible company secretarial support. Whether you are a small private company or a larger corporate structure, our services adapt to meet your needs.

services.title: Our Core Company Secretarial Services Include
paragraphs: Maintaining and updating statutory registers for shareholders, directors, and PSCs
Preparing and filing Confirmation Statements
Providing a prestigious London registered office and, optionally, acting as your appointed corporate secretary

services.title: Statutory Registers and Persons with Significant Control (PSC) Management
paragraphs: Every company must have a registered office for statutory notices and legal correspondence. Axsuma provides a prestigious London address that appears on all Companies House filings, public documents, and statutory notices. We receive all statutory correspondence, including documents from Companies House (such as confirmation statement reminders and annual filing notices) and HMRC communications (tax assessments, penalties, enquiries).
Our mail forwarding service includes email notifications with scanned copies of all mail received, ensuring you stay fully informed without the need for a physical office presence.

The Companies Act 2006 requires companies to maintain detailed statutory registers: the register of members (shareholders), register of directors, register of directors' residential addresses, register of secretaries, register of charges (mortgages/security interests), and (since 2016) the register of persons with significant control. These registers must be accurate, current, and available for inspection by members and (for PSC information) the public.

We maintain your statutory registers, ensuring they reflect current shareholdings, director changes, charges (such as bank security interests), and PSC information. When directors change (appointments, resignations, retirements), we update the directors register, file notifications with Companies House (Forms AP01 for appointments, TM01 for resignations), and manage director address confidentiality (filing private registers for residential addresses).

The PSC register records beneficial owners—individuals who ultimately own or control 25% or more of the company's shares or voting power. PSC identification has become increasingly important for anti-money laundering compliance, sanctions screening, and beneficial ownership transparency. We identify PSC holders, collect beneficial ownership information, verify PSC documentation, and maintain the PSC register with Companies House, filing PSC notifications (Forms PSC01-PSC08) within statutory 14-day deadlines.

For companies with nominee shareholders or complex ownership structures, we manage shareholder register accuracy while maintaining confidentiality of beneficial owners. For companies acquiring other companies or undertaking restructurings, we update registers to reflect new shareholdings, transfers of shares, and changes in corporate structure. All register updates are timely, accurate, and compliant with Companies House requirements.

services.title: Transfer-In and Migration from Other Providers
paragraphs: Every company (with limited exemptions) must file a Confirmation Statement with Companies House at least once every 12 months, typically coinciding with the company's accounting reference date. The Confirmation Statement confirms that the company's statutory registers (directors, members, charges, PSC) are accurate as of the confirmation date. Failure to file confirmation statements results in penalties escalating from £150 to £750+, potential striking off the register, and director disqualification.

We prepare and file Confirmation Statements on your behalf, gathering required information: confirmation of director details and changes, shareholder register accuracy, PSC information updates, charge register accuracy (including confirmation of security interests and liens), and registered office confirmation. We file Confirmation Statements within statutory deadlines (14 days after the due date incurs a £150 penalty; more than 3 months incurs £750+). For companies in financial distress, timely confirmation statement filing can be the difference between company survival and striking off.

We also manage other statutory filings: accounts and reports filing (for companies above the small company threshold), directors' reports, auditors' reports (if required), and changes to corporate structure (mergers, capital changes, share issuances). We ensure all filings are accurate, timely, and comply with Companies House filing requirements and the Companies Act 2006.

For companies undergoing changes—acquisitions, mergers, restructuring, management changes—we coordinate all necessary statutory filings to reflect the company's evolving structure. We maintain a comprehensive filing calendar ensuring no statutory deadlines are missed and all Companies House obligations remain current.

If your company is currently managed by another provider, Axsuma can seamlessly take over company secretarial support. We coordinate the transition, retrieve all statutory information, verify register accuracy, identify pending filings, and assume responsibility for ongoing governance and compliance.

During the transfer-in process, we conduct a comprehensive compliance review: ensuring confirmation statements are up to date, verifying accounts are properly filed, checking director and PSC details for accuracy, and confirming charges are correctly registered. Any gaps or regulatory exposure are addressed with a remediation plan to bring your company fully into compliance.

why_cards.title: Deadline Reliability
why_cards.description: Zero missed deadlines. We maintain a comprehensive filing calendar ensuring every confirmation statement, PSC notification, and statutory filing is timely and accurate. No penalties, no striking off.

why_cards.title: Comprehensive Compliance
why_cards.description: Beyond basic filings—we ensure your statutory registers are accurate, PSC information is current, director details are correct, and board governance is compliant. Full governance oversight.

why_cards.title: Seamless Transitions
why_cards.description: Transferring from another provider? We coordinate transitions, verify compliance, identify gaps, and ensure no disruption to your governance framework or filing obligations.

cta.title: Ensure Your Company Stays Compliant
cta.body: Whether establishing secretarial support, transferring from another provider, or resolving compliance gaps, we provide the governance framework your company needs.

header.title: M&A Transaction Services
header.subtitle: Readiness solutions for complex corporate transactions. UK ready-made packages for M&A, restructuring, and joint ventures.

overview.title: Readiness Solutions for Complex Corporate Transactions
paragraphs: In the fast-paced environment of complex corporate transactions, timing is critical. Complex deal structures, regulatory hurdles, and aggressive timelines mean there is zero margin for administrative delay. We know that as dealmakers, your focus belongs on negotiation, strategy, and execution — not on the distraction of the corporate administration aspects.

Axsuma streamlines the process. We support lawyers, corporates, and private equity firms with pre-established corporate structures purpose-built for fast-moving M&A, restructuring, and carve-outs. We handle the essential administrative groundwork, providing the speed and structural certainty required to close the deal. Beyond completion, we ensure all entities remain fully compliant across their regulatory, accounting, tax, and payroll obligations.

We offer ready-made HoldCo, MidCo, and BidCo entities available for immediate reservation. These structures are securely held to order exclusively for law firms and advisors, allowing you to focus on deal structuring and negotiation without administrative bottlenecks.

services.title: Transaction-Ready Corporate Stacks
paragraphs: Accelerate deal execution with pre-established structures. Delays in entity formation, governance setup, or appointing directors can create unnecessary friction during sensitive deal phases. Our solution eliminates these risks.

We offer ready-made HoldCo, MidCo, and BidCo entities available for immediate reservation. These structures are securely held to order exclusively for law firms and advisors, allowing you to focus on deal structuring and negotiation without administrative bottlenecks.

The Ready-Made Corporate Stack: Our fully aligned three-tier acquisition structure is configurable to specific transaction requirements. All entities are established with clean corporate records, dormant status, and are maintained in good standing. HoldCo: ultimate parent entity; structured to support ownership, financing, and long-term governance. MidCo: intermediary holding entity; designed for structural flexibility and risk segregation. BidCo: acquisition vehicle; purpose-built to execute the purchase and receive assets or shares at closing.

services.title: How It Works: Reservation & Hold-to-Order
paragraphs: Immediate Reservation - Entities can be reserved instantly upon instruction, securing exclusivity for your transaction.
Held to Order for Up to 6 Months (extendable by agreement) - We maintain the corporate stack on your behalf - dormant, compliant, and ready - while deal negotiations progress.
Activation on Demand - Upon confirmation, entities are fully activated and transferred in accordance with your instructions and transaction timeline.

services.title: Designed for Lawyers & Transaction Advisors
paragraphs: We operate as an infrastructure partner, not a legal decision-maker – supporting your work with speed and precision. Our offering is purpose-built for:
Lawyers managing complex and time-sensitive M&A transactions
Private equity and investment advisors
Corporate development and in-house legal teams; Cross-border and multi-jurisdictional deal structures

services.title: Key Benefits
paragraphs: Reduced time to signing and closing
Predictable transaction readiness
Clean, pre-established corporate records
Flexible holding period aligned with deal timelines; Confidential and controlled reservation process

services.title: Single SPVs
paragraphs: If you are seeking a sole SPV for an upcoming transaction, we also retain standalone entities for immediate reservation on the same held to order basis.
Transaction-Ready. When You Need It.
Whether preparing for a competitive bid, conditional signing, or regulatory approval phase, our ready-made HoldCo–MidCo–BidCo stacks give you the structural certainty to move decisively.
It will be necessary to conduct some compliance checks in accordance with the UK Money Laundering Regulations upon transfer of ownership at completion.

why_cards.title: Deep Transactional Experience
why_cards.description: Hundreds of acquisitions, disposals, and complex restructurings. We understand deal mechanics, completion procedures, and integration challenges across industries and deal sizes.

why_cards.title: Flawless Deal Execution
why_cards.description: Board resolutions, completion accounts, statutory filings, and post-completion governance managed with meticulous attention to detail. No closing delays, no corporate gaps.

why_cards.title: Strategic Transaction Advice
why_cards.description: Beyond mechanics—we advise on acquisition structure, holding company setup, SPV segregation, and integration strategy. Your corporate structure drives value.

cta.title: Ready to Execute Your Acquisition or Disposal?
cta.body: Discuss your transaction structure, completion timeline, and governance requirements. We'll guide you through every stage from deal structure through post-completion integration.

header.title: SPV & Holding Company Services
header.subtitle: Specialist structuring for acquisitions, holdings, and complex group arrangements. Built for M&A, private equity, and strategic transactions.

overview.title: Strategic Corporate Structuring
paragraphs: Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) and holding company structures are fundamental to modern M&A transactions, private equity acquisitions, and multi-entity group arrangements. Proper structuring ensures tax efficiency, risk segregation, operational clarity, and regulatory compliance. We provide specialist guidance on establishment, governance, and integration of SPVs, HoldCos, MidCos, and BidCos into your corporate landscape.

Whether you are establishing acquisition vehicles for a transaction, creating holding company structures for group management, or segregating operational risk through SPVs, we manage the complete corporate lifecycle—from formation and governance setup through ongoing compliance and statutory management.

services.title: SPV Establishment & Management
paragraphs: Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)—narrow-purpose companies established for specific transactions or operational functions—require careful governance and statutory management. Whether establishing an SPV to isolate operational risk, segregate assets, facilitate financings, or hold specific business units, we manage the SPV's complete corporate lifecycle.

SPVs are commonly used to acquire specific assets or business lines, to facilitate management buyouts, to isolate pension liabilities, or to separate operational activities from parent company risk. The SPV structure must comply with the Companies Act 2006, maintain arm's length transfer pricing, preserve the substance of the SPV's operations (avoiding permanent establishment issues if it's a foreign SPV), and maintain corporate separation through proper capitalisation, board governance, and statutory filing.

We establish the SPV with appropriate articles of association, capitalise it with parent company funding (via equity investment or intercompany loans), and establish governance protocols ensuring independent board decision-making and statutory compliance. We prepare acquisition structures where the SPV acquires the target's assets or business, transfer pricing documentation for intercompany charges, and management accounts demonstrating the SPV's financial independence.

Upon acquisition, the SPV becomes the operating company or holding company for the acquired business. We manage its statutory registers, ensure its annual confirmation statements reflect the actual business structure, manage intercompany agreements between the SPV and parent company, and coordinate the SPV's integration into the wider group governance.

services.title: Holding Company Structuring
paragraphs: Holding companies serve as parent entities managing subsidiary companies, operational companies, and asset structures. Proper holding company structuring is essential for tax efficiency, regulatory compliance, dividend cascade control, and corporate governance. We establish holding company structures tailored to your group's operational needs and financial structure.

Holding companies under English law (section 1159 Companies Act 2006) must have subsidiaries where the parent company can exercise dominant influence and hold at least 50% of subsidiary shares. Holding company exemptions from preparing consolidated accounts, group accounts structures, and dividend payment limitations require careful statutory compliance. We ensure your holding company is properly registered as such with Companies House and maintains documentation demonstrating its status.

We establish holding company articles reflecting multi-subsidiary governance requirements, manage shareholder agreements between holding company and subsidiary operators, and coordinate group-wide board decision-making. Where the holding company is a foreign entity, we establish a UK registered office and ensure compliance with overseas company registration requirements.

Holding company structures often involve managing cash flows—consolidating profits from operating subsidiaries, distributing dividends, managing intercompany loan arrangements, and optimising tax efficiency across the group. We prepare the holding company's statutory accounts reflecting subsidiary valuations, manage its annual filing obligations, and advise on corporate restructuring as the group evolves.

services.title: MidCo, BidCo & Acquisition Stacks
paragraphs: Acquisition structures frequently employ three-tier stacks comprising HoldCo (ultimate parent), MidCo (intermediary holding entity), and BidCo (acquisition vehicle). This structure provides flexibility, risk segregation, and structural optionality for investors, lenders, and target sellers. HoldCo serves as the ultimate parent entity, structured to support ownership, financing, and long-term governance. MidCo acts as an intermediary holding entity designed for structural flexibility and risk segregation. BidCo is the acquisition vehicle, purpose-built to execute the purchase and receive assets or shares at closing.

We establish transaction-ready HoldCo, MidCo, and BidCo entities available for immediate reservation. All entities are established with clean corporate records, dormant status, and maintained in good standing. This allows deal teams to focus on transaction negotiation without administrative bottlenecks. Entities can be reserved instantly upon instruction, securing exclusivity for your transaction. We maintain the corporate stack on your behalf—dormant, compliant, and ready—while deal negotiations progress, held to order for up to 6 months (extendable by agreement).

Upon confirmation, entities are fully activated and transferred in accordance with your instructions and transaction timeline. Acquisition stacks require careful structuring around debt/equity arrangements, working capital adjustments, earn-outs, and seller rollover arrangements. We manage the entire stack lifecycle from reservation through post-completion integration, ensuring all entities remain compliant and properly documented.

why_cards.title: Structural Expertise
why_cards.description: Deep experience across SPV architectures, multi-tier holding structures, acquisition stacks, and complex group arrangements. We understand the tax, legal, and commercial implications of every structure.

why_cards.title: Seamless Integration
why_cards.description: Your SPVs and holding companies don't exist in isolation. We integrate them into your wider governance framework, ensuring consistent compliance and coordinated management across the group.

why_cards.title: Transaction-Ready Speed
why_cards.description: Pre-established, dormant entities available for immediate reservation. No delays in deal execution. We hold entities to order while negotiations progress, ready to activate on your timeline.

cta.title: Discuss Your Structuring Needs
cta.body: Whether planning an acquisition, establishing a holding company, or optimising your group structure, we'll guide you through the options and establish the architecture that serves your objectives.

header.title: Professional Directorship Services
header.subtitle: Independent, experienced directors for SPVs, holding companies, and family offices. Governance credibility where it matters.

overview.title: Independent Board Oversight for Complex Structures
paragraphs: In many acquisitions, private equity investments, and complex corporate structures, professional directors bring essential governance credibility and independent oversight. Companies require directors—not just corporate secretaries—to ensure statutory compliance, manage conflicts of interest, and provide fiduciary duty oversight. We provide professional directors (both executive and non-executive) who join company boards to deliver the governance discipline that investors, lenders, and stakeholders expect.

This is particularly valuable in management buyouts, institutional investments, multi-tier holding structures, or where management team members have competing interests. A professional director—independent, experienced, and accountable—ensures the board makes informed decisions with proper oversight, that statutory obligations are met, and that the company maintains governance credibility through transition periods and ongoing operations.

services.title: Independent & Non-Executive Directors
paragraphs: Professional directors ensure the company has independent board scrutiny of major decisions and compliance with the Companies Act 2006 director duties (care, diligence, good faith, independent judgment, and avoiding conflicts). Our professional directors have extensive experience in M&A transactions, post-acquisition integration, financial restructuring, and corporate governance across sectors and deal sizes.

As non-executive or independent directors, they bring objectivity to board decision-making, challenge management assumptions, ensure proper financial controls, and provide investor confidence. In acquisition scenarios, they serve as the neutral party overseeing completion mechanics, managing earnout determinations, and ensuring post-completion integration remains compliant. For holding companies and fund structures, they provide the governance oversight that limited partners and institutional investors require.

Professional directors also manage conflicts of interest that arise in complex transactions. Where selling shareholders roll equity into the buyer, where management teams have competing interests, or where institutional investors have board representation, professional directors manage conflict disclosure procedures and ensure decisions are made with proper independent oversight.

services.title: SPV & Acquisition Vehicle Directors
paragraphs: Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) and acquisition vehicles (BidCos) require careful governance to ensure the structure delivers its intended purpose and maintains corporate separation from the parent. We provide directors specifically for SPV and acquisition structures, ensuring these entities maintain independent board governance, proper record-keeping, and statutory compliance.

SPV directors oversee the execution of the specific transaction or function the SPV is established for—whether acquisition of assets, segregation of operational risk, or holding of specific business units. They ensure the SPV maintains arm's length transfer pricing with parent company entities, that intercompany agreements are properly documented, and that the SPV's separate corporate existence is preserved through proper capitalisation, board governance, and statutory filing.

For acquisition vehicles in multi-tier structures (HoldCo/MidCo/BidCo), directors manage the execution of the acquisition, completion mechanics, working capital adjustments, and post-completion integration. They oversee earnout satisfaction determinations, manage any seller indemnity claims, and ensure the acquired business is properly integrated into the wider group governance framework.

services.title: Holding Company & Fund Governance Directors
paragraphs: Holding companies managing multiple subsidiary companies and fund structures require directors who understand multi-entity governance, consolidated group reporting, and investor relations. Professional directors for holding companies manage group-wide strategic decisions, oversee subsidiary performance and integration, and ensure the group structure operates with proper governance disciplines.

For funds and family offices, directors provide the governance credibility that limited partners and institutional investors expect. They oversee investment decisions, monitor performance against fund objectives, manage distribution mechanics, and ensure compliance with fund documentation and investment guidelines. Where fund structures involve multiple tiers (GP companies, GP-led secondaries, or continuation vehicles), professional directors manage governance across the structure.

Holding company directors also facilitate communication between parent company boards and subsidiary management teams, oversee dividend distribution mechanics, manage cash flow coordination across the group, and ensure consolidated group reporting is accurate and timely. In family office structures, they provide the independent governance that protects family assets and ensures long-term stewardship.

services.title: Resident & Corporate Directors
paragraphs: Companies are required to have at least one director who is a natural person resident in the UK. Foreign entities establishing UK operations, overseas-controlled SPVs, and private equity firms establishing acquisition structures often require local, resident directors to satisfy this statutory requirement. We provide qualified resident directors who maintain a UK residential address complying with Companies House requirements.

Corporate directors offer an additional governance option. Rather than appointing an individual director, a company can appoint a corporate director (itself a company). This is useful where directorship responsibility needs to be segregated within a group structure or where a sponsor company wants to maintain governance control without individual director appointment. We establish and manage corporate director entities on behalf of clients.

Both resident and corporate director appointments involve proper statutory notification to Companies House, including director appointment forms (Form AP01), residential address filings, and maintenance of statutory registers. We manage all director appointment procedures, ensuring regulatory compliance and documentation accuracy.

services.title: Director Administration & Statutory Compliance
paragraphs: Beyond board governance, professional directors require comprehensive administrative support to discharge their statutory duties. We manage all director-related compliance: director appointment and resignation procedures, preparation of board meeting minutes reflecting statutory decision-making, management of director appointment forms (Form AP01) and resignation forms (Form TM01), and maintenance of statutory registers reflecting current director details and addresses.

Directors are required to file residential address information with Companies House, with the option to maintain a private register for residential addresses. We manage director address confidentiality filing, ensuring sensitive residential information is protected while remaining compliant with statutory requirements. When directors change (appointments, resignations, retirements), we coordinate all notification procedures and statutory filings.

We also coordinate directors' disqualification checks, conflict of interest management, director indemnity insurance arrangements, and attendance at board meetings. Our professional directors provide detailed board minutes documenting decisions, authorisations, and governance actions, ensuring the company maintains a clear record of board governance and statutory compliance.

why_cards.title: Experienced & Credible
why_cards.description: Our directors bring extensive experience in M&A transactions, corporate governance, financial restructuring, and investor relations. They provide the credibility that boards and investors expect.

why_cards.title: Impartial Governance
why_cards.description: Independent, objective oversight. Our professional directors challenge assumptions, manage conflicts of interest, and ensure decisions are made with proper governance discipline.

why_cards.title: Fully Integrated Support
why_cards.description: Directors are not isolated individuals. We provide administrative support, statutory compliance, board coordination, and integration with your wider governance framework.

cta.title: Appoint Professional Directors for Your Structure
cta.body: Discuss your governance requirements with our team. We'll match you with the right director for your entity, structure, and stage of development.

header.title: Accounting & Tax Services
header.subtitle: A unified, scalable solution designed to simplify compliance, enhance visibility, and support better financial decision-making across jurisdictions and structures.

overview.title: Streamlined Finance & Tax Functions
paragraphs: Finance and tax functions are often fragmented across multiple providers, creating inefficiency, inconsistent reporting, and unnecessary administrative burden. We deliver a unified, scalable solution designed to simplify compliance, enhance visibility, and support better financial decision-making across jurisdictions and structures.

Our services include: Statutory accounts preparation and financial reporting in line with relevant accounting standards. Corporate tax compliance, filings, and advisory support, including optimisation of reliefs and allowances. Monthly and quarterly management accounts with tailored, multi-entity and multi-currency reporting. Outsourced finance functions, including bookkeeping, payroll, and VAT compliance.

services.title: Bookkeeping & Monthly Accounting
paragraphs: Monthly bookkeeping ensures your financial records are accurate and current. We process invoices, record expenses, reconcile bank statements, manage payables and receivables, and maintain a complete audit trail of financial transactions. Every transaction is categorized correctly for tax purposes, with proper supporting documentation and approval workflows.

We manage your general ledger, bank reconciliations, and subsidiary ledgers (debtors, creditors, fixed assets, inventory). Monthly bank reconciliations catch errors early, identify reconciling items, and ensure your accounting records match your actual bank position. For companies with multiple bank accounts or complex cash flows, we coordinate all accounts through a consolidated cash management system.

Bookkeeping also includes management of invoicing and receivables. We ensure customer invoices are issued timely, recorded in the sales ledger, and tracked through aging analysis. We identify overdue receivables, manage payment collections, and forecast cash flow based on receivables patterns. Similarly, we manage purchase invoices, record supplier payments, maintain a creditor aging analysis, and coordinate payment schedules with cash flow forecasts.

We provide monthly reporting—your company's profit and loss statement, balance sheet, and key performance metrics—enabling you to track business performance month-by-month. These management accounts become the foundation for tax planning, bank covenant reporting, and strategic decision-making. All bookkeeping is conducted using cloud-based accounting software with automated bank feeds, reducing data entry errors and improving timeliness.

services.title: VAT Returns, Compliance & HMRC Management
paragraphs: Every company must file a Corporation Tax return with HMRC within 12 months of the end of its accounting period. The return includes the company's profit calculation, tax adjustments, allowable deductions (capital allowances, interest, trading losses), and tax liability. HMRC uses submitted returns to set tax payment deadlines, trigger enquiry processes, and assess penalties for late or inaccurate filing.

We prepare corporation tax returns (CT600 forms) from your accounting records, applying HMRC rules for trading expenses, capital allowances, interest deductibility, and loss relief. We identify tax-efficient deductions your business qualifies for, ensuring you don't pay tax on profits you're not entitled to. We claim capital allowances for equipment and assets (plant and machinery allowances, industrial buildings allowances, R&D tax credits where applicable), maximizing tax relief for capital investment.

Tax returns must be filed within statutory deadlines to avoid penalties. Companies filing returns after the deadline face penalties of £100+ for first failure, escalating to £300+ for subsequent failures, plus interest. Making Tax Digital (MTD) requirements mean many companies must file returns and keep digital records. We ensure your filing is timely, accurate, and compliant with HMRC's MTD standards.

We also manage tax planning throughout the year, identifying opportunities to structure transactions tax-efficiently. This includes advising on dividends versus salary optimization for owner-managers, timing of capital investments for tax relief, management of trading losses, and intercompany transactions (where applicable for group companies). Tax planning is not about avoiding tax illegally; it's about structuring legitimate transactions efficiently within the law.

VAT (Value Added Tax) is a complex tax affecting companies with turnover above the £85,000 threshold. VAT-registered companies must file VAT returns (typically quarterly) showing output tax (VAT charged to customers), input tax (VAT paid to suppliers), and net VAT payable to HMRC or credit from HMRC. VAT returns must be filed within one month and seven days of the quarter end.

We manage your VAT compliance: maintaining proper VAT records (invoices, receipts, and VAT accounts), calculating output and input tax correctly, filing VAT returns on time, and managing VAT payments to HMRC. We ensure all eligible input tax is claimed (including acquisitions of goods and services), maximizing VAT recovery while maintaining compliance with HMRC rules on blocked input tax (for example, VAT on fuel for personal use cannot be recovered).

We also advise on VAT optimization. Some supplies are VAT-exempt (making it difficult to recover VAT incurred), while others qualify for zero-rating (allowing full VAT recovery). Structuring transactions correctly—for example, the difference between supplying goods versus services, domestic versus export supplies—can dramatically affect VAT treatment. We advise on exemptions and zero-rating opportunities, protecting your company from overpaying VAT while maintaining compliance.

We manage VAT recovery for import/export transactions, cross-border services, and EU/international business. We handle HMRC VAT enquiries, support HMRC visits, prepare supporting documentation, and negotiate with HMRC on VAT assessments. Late VAT returns trigger penalties of 5-15% of unpaid VAT, with additional interest, making timely filing essential.

services.title: PAYE & Payroll Management
paragraphs: Monthly or quarterly management accounts provide current visibility into your business's financial performance. Management accounts show your profit and loss (revenue, gross profit, operating expenses, EBITDA), balance sheet (assets, liabilities, equity), and cash flow statement. Unlike statutory accounts (filed with Companies House after year-end), management accounts are timely and support day-to-day business management.

We produce detailed management accounts with variance analysis (comparing actual results against budget or prior periods), profitability analysis by division/product/customer, working capital reporting (inventory, receivables, payables levels), and cash flow forecasts. Many companies adjust price, costs, or strategy based on management accounting insights. A business without current financial visibility is essentially flying blind—management accounts eliminate that risk.

For companies with loan facilities, management accounts often form part of bank covenant reporting. Banks typically require quarterly management accounts showing compliance with financial covenants (for example, minimum EBITDA, maximum debt-to-EBITDA ratios, minimum interest coverage). We prepare management accounts to bank reporting standards, showing full compliance or highlighting any covenant concerns early enough to address them with your lender.

We also advise investors and stakeholders based on financial reporting. Investor boards require detailed financial and operational reporting to monitor investment performance. We prepare investor reports, manage data rooms for fundraising or M&A, and coordinate financial due diligence processes.

Companies with employees must operate PAYE (Pay As You Earn), deducting income tax and National Insurance contributions from employee salaries and remitting these to HMRC. PAYE compliance includes calculating employee tax and NI correctly, issuing payslips, filing monthly PAYE returns (Real Time Information—RTI submissions), and managing end-of-year tax coding and P60 documents.

We manage your payroll from start to finish: setting up payroll systems, calculating salaries and deductions, processing employee payments, managing pension contributions, and maintaining employee records. We ensure statutory deductions are correct (income tax bands, National Insurance, student loan repayments where applicable), voluntary deductions are processed (pension contributions, charitable giving), and statutory maternity/paternity/adoption pay is paid correctly.

We file Real Time Information (RTI) returns with HMRC—when we pay employees, we notify HMRC of pay and deductions. RTI simplifies HMRC's monitoring of PAYE compliance and reduces year-end adjustments. We also manage employer National Insurance contributions (which are separate from employee NI), claiming any available reliefs (for example, employment allowance for small employers, startup NI relief).

We advise on tax-efficient structuring of remuneration: the mix of salary versus dividends/pension contributions for owner-managed businesses, optimization of benefits-in-kind versus salary (for example, company cars versus cash), and compliance with National Living Wage requirements. We also manage employee tax documentation (P45s for leavers, P60s for tax return purposes) and coordinate with HMRC on payroll queries or audits.

services.title: ATED Returns & Property Tax Compliance
paragraphs: Companies holding UK residential property valued above £500,000 must file Annual Tax on Enveloped Dwellings (ATED) returns with HMRC. ATED was introduced to ensure UK residential property owned by companies pays tax on the property, preventing tax-avoidance schemes. ATED applies to companies, trusts, and foreign entities holding residential properties over the £500,000 threshold, regardless of whether the property generates rental income.

ATED returns must be filed by April 30th each year, showing the property's valuation, relief claims (where the property qualifies for relief—for example, properties under renovation), and ATED tax liability. Annual ATED returns range from £3,500 (properties valued £500k-£1m) to £19,500+ (properties valued over £20m). Late ATED filing triggers penalties starting at £100 per month of delay.

We calculate ATED liability based on property valuations, identify any available reliefs (properties under substantial renovation, protected buildings, properties occupied as only/main residences by non-UK persons), prepare and file ATED returns by the statutory deadline, and manage ATED payments to HMRC. We also coordinate with your property advisors on valuations and ensure ATED compliance doesn't conflict with property tax structuring.

Beyond ATED, companies holding property may be subject to Stamp Duty Land Tax (SDLT) on purchase, Land Value Tax considerations (for future implementations), and Council Tax if the property is residential. We advise on property ownership structures that minimize tax burden while maintaining legitimate business purposes and compliance with anti-avoidance rules.

why_cards.title: HMRC Compliance
why_cards.description: No missed deadlines, no inaccurate returns, no penalties. We ensure your corporation tax, VAT, PAYE, and ATED obligations are always current and compliant with HMRC's exacting standards.

why_cards.title: Tax Efficiency
why_cards.description: We identify tax-saving opportunities—capital allowances, relief claims, optimal structuring. Legitimate tax planning reduces your tax bill while maintaining full compliance.

why_cards.title: Business Insight
why_cards.description: Detailed management accounts and financial reporting help you understand profitability, cash flow, and performance. Data-driven decisions drive better business outcomes.

cta.title: Let's Get Your Finances in Order
cta.body: From startup bookkeeping through to complex group tax planning, we provide comprehensive accounting and tax compliance tailored to your business.

header.title: Payroll & HR Services
header.subtitle: Streamlined payroll processing and HR administration that ensures compliance and supports your growing UK workforce.

overview.title: Compliant, Coordinated People Operations
paragraphs: Payroll and HR management is increasingly complex, driven by evolving legislation, real-time reporting requirements, and heightened expectations around accuracy and employee experience. We provide a streamlined solution that reduces administrative burden while ensuring full compliance with HMRC and employment regulations.

Our approach combines precise payroll delivery with practical HR support, giving you a single, coordinated service that integrates seamlessly with your wider operations. Whether you are managing a growing UK workforce or supporting inbound entities, we deliver consistency, control, and clear visibility into your people operations.

services.title: Payroll Processing & Administration
paragraphs: We provide end-to-end payroll delivery, including calculations, payslips, and payment coordination. From employee onboarding through to final settlement, we manage all elements of the payroll cycle with precision and compliance. Our systems integrate with modern HR platforms, allowing you to maintain full visibility into your payroll processing.

Our payroll service includes: accurate calculation of gross pay, statutory deductions, and voluntary contributions; preparation and distribution of payslips to employees; processing of variation to payroll (pay changes, arrears, adjustments); management of statutory payments and allowances; and coordination of payment runs to employees, HMRC, and pension providers.

We manage payroll reconciliation and reporting, including year-end final settlement procedures, tax code changes, and statutory reporting to HMRC. We also coordinate with external payroll providers where you maintain in-house payroll resources, ensuring consistency and reducing duplicate processing.

services.title: HMRC Compliance & Real Time Information (RTI) Reporting
paragraphs: Management of PAYE, National Insurance, and all Real Time Information (RTI) submissions is critical to payroll compliance. We manage the complete HMRC reporting cycle: calculation and payment of PAYE tax deductions; management of National Insurance contributions (employee and employer contributions); submission of Full Payment Submission (FPS) files to HMRC on or before each pay day; and reconciliation of year-end tax and National Insurance returns.

RTI reporting requires submission of payroll data to HMRC each payday, with HMRC matching reported income against employees' tax coding notices. We manage FPS submissions, ensure timely payment of PAYE and National Insurance to HMRC, handle Employment Allowance claims where eligible, and coordinate with HMRC for any formal inquiries or adjustments.

We also manage year-end RTI reconciliation, including submission of year-end adjustments if payroll figures require amendment following year-end closure. For companies in multiple jurisdictions, we coordinate payroll reporting across different HMRC requirements and tax authority processes.

services.title: Auto-Enrolment & Workplace Pensions
paragraphs: Administration of workplace pension schemes, including enrolment, contributions and compliance. Employers with payroll are automatically subject to auto-enrolment obligations, requiring the establishment of a suitable workplace pension scheme and auto-enrolment of eligible employees. We manage all elements of auto-enrolment compliance.

We coordinate establishment of qualifying pension schemes, manage the auto-enrolment process including identification of eligible employees (aged 22 and above, earning £12,570 or above annually), processing of enrolment election notices, and coordination of employee communications. We maintain compliance with the Pensions Regulator's requirements, including keeping enrolment records and managing opt-in/opt-out requests.

We also process pension contributions from payroll, ensuring employer contributions (minimum 8% of qualifying earnings) and employee contributions are correctly deducted and remitted to pension providers. We manage employee communications regarding pension scheme benefits, provide guidance on pension planning, and coordinate with pension scheme trustees on ongoing compliance matters.

services.title: HR Administration Support
paragraphs: Employee onboarding and offboarding, contracts of employment, and maintenance of personnel records. We provide HR administration support coordinated with payroll processing, ensuring new employees are correctly set up on payroll systems and departing employees are properly processed for final settlement.

We prepare employment contracts reflecting company policy, statutory requirements, and role-specific terms. We manage the onboarding process including right-to-work verification, NI number registration, tax code confirmation, and setup on payroll systems. For departing employees, we process exit procedures, final payroll settlement, and coordination of final tax documentation (P45, holiday pay settlement).

We maintain personnel records including employment contracts, payroll history, tax documentation, and statutory information. We manage employee changes (salary increases, promotions, department changes) and ensure payroll systems remain aligned with current employee status. We also provide guidance on HR processes, employment law compliance, and best practice employment policies.

services.title: Statutory Payments & Employee Leave Management
paragraphs: Management of statutory sick pay (SSP), maternity/paternity pay and associated entitlements. When employees are absent due to sickness or family circumstances, statutory payments ensure employees receive continuity of income while complying with employment law requirements.

We manage statutory sick pay administration, including certification of employee absence, calculation of SSP at the statutory rate (currently £111.35 per week), and coordination with occupational sick pay schemes where companies supplement statutory minimums. For maternity, paternity, and parental leave, we manage statutory maternity pay (SMP) and paternity pay (SPP) calculations, ensuring eligible employees receive statutory payments at the required rates.

We also manage holiday pay calculations and accrual, ensure compliance with working time regulations (minimum 28 days paid holiday), and coordinate holiday pay settlement for departing employees. We provide guidance on managing employee leave requests, statutory payment entitlements, and employment policies around absence management.

services.title: Advisory & Best Practice Guidance
paragraphs: Guidance on UK employment processes, policies and operational efficiency. Employment law is complex and constantly evolving. We provide practical advice on compliance with employment law, best practice HR policies, and operational efficiency improvements.

We advise on employment contract drafting, disciplinary and grievance procedures, redundancy management, flexible working requests, and discrimination and harassment policies. We coordinate with employment law specialists where formal legal advice is required, and we support employees and managers through complex employment situations.

We also advise on payroll efficiency improvements, integration of payroll systems with HR processes, and coordination with accounting and tax compliance. Our advisory service is designed to support your business as you grow and evolve your people operations.

why_cards.title: Compliance Certainty
why_cards.description: HMRC compliance, RTI reporting, auto-enrolment, statutory payments—we manage the complete regulatory framework so you can focus on your business.

why_cards.title: Integrated Services
why_cards.description: Payroll, HR, and accounting work together. Our coordinated approach ensures consistency across people operations and financial reporting.

why_cards.title: Scalable Support
why_cards.description: From first hire through growing workforce, we scale our support with your business. Transparent pricing, no hidden costs.

cta.title: Streamline Your Payroll & HR Operations
cta.body: Discuss your people operations with our team. We'll design a coordinated payroll and HR support structure that fits your business and scales with growth.

header.title: Compliance & Governance Services
header.subtitle: Specialist regulatory compliance and governance support. Overseas entities, process agent services, ID verification, and more.

overview.title: Governance Framework Designed for Growth
paragraphs: We support organisations in establishing and operating robust, proportionate governance frameworks that stand up to regulatory scrutiny and scale with growth. Our focus is practical delivery—ensuring governance is not only compliant, but embedded, efficient, and aligned to the realities of how your business operates.

We work with companies, funds, family offices and their advisers across a range of jurisdictions, often in complex or fast-moving environments. Whether you require targeted advisory input, interim support, or a fully outsourced company secretarial function, our model is designed to integrate seamlessly and deliver with precision.

Our compliance and governance services span company secretarial support, statutory filings, board and meeting management, interim and outsourced company secretary functions, and specialist regulatory services including overseas entity registration, process agent services, and ID verification.

services.title: Company Secretarial Services
services.description: End-to-end support including statutory filings, annual compliance, provision of named Company Secretary, and comprehensive governance management for UK companies.

services.title: Register of Overseas Entities (ROE)
services.description: Compliance support for overseas entities owning UK property. Registration, beneficial ownership verification, and ongoing filing obligations.

services.title: Process Agent Services
services.description: UK-based legal representation and service of process for overseas companies and foreign entities operating in the UK.

services.title: ID Verification Services
services.description: Enhanced due diligence and identity verification for directors, beneficial owners, and key individuals supporting regulatory compliance.

services.title: UK Address for Service (IP)
services.description: Designated UK address for intellectual property matters. Receipt and forwarding of IP correspondence and formal notices.

services.title: Board & Meeting Support
services.description: Coordination and delivery of board, committee and shareholder meetings including agendas, papers, minutes and governance documentation.

cta.title: Establish Your Compliance Framework
cta.body: Whether navigating overseas entity registration, process agent requirements, or comprehensive governance setup, we'll guide you through regulatory requirements and establish a compliance structure tailored to your business.

header.title: Overseas Entities & Beneficial Ownership
header.subtitle: Register of Overseas Entities (ROE) compliance and beneficial ownership verification under the Economic Crime Act 2022.

overview.title: Beneficial Ownership Transparency & ROE Compliance
paragraphs: The Economic Crime Act 2022, introduced following recommendations from the National Crime Agency and Financial Action Task Force, requires overseas entities that own or acquire property in the UK to register with Companies House and disclose their beneficial owners. The Register of Overseas Entities (ROE) represents a fundamental shift toward transparency—foreign companies and their true beneficial owners are now part of the public record, preventing anonymous property ownership by corrupt officials, sanctions evaders, and money launderers.

For overseas entities, ROE registration is now mandatory before acquiring or holding UK property. Failure to register incurs significant penalties: up to £2,500 per day for unregistered entities (up to £20,000 cumulative), and overseas entities cannot hold property in their own name without registration. Beneficial owners of registered overseas entities must be accurately identified, with ongoing reporting obligations if beneficial ownership structures change.

We guide overseas entities through ROE registration, collect beneficial ownership information, verify identity documentation, and manage ongoing reporting obligations. Our international compliance expertise ensures your entity meets UK transparency requirements while maintaining any legitimate offshore structures for tax or operational purposes.

services.title: What is the Register of Overseas Entities (ROE)
paragraphs: The Register of Overseas Entities is Companies House's public register of overseas entities that own or hold UK property. Introduced under the Economic Crime Act 2022, the ROE is part of the UK's anti-money laundering and sanctions compliance framework. Every overseas entity (including foreign companies, trusts, and partnerships) holding property in the UK must be registered on the ROE with disclosed beneficial owners.

Beneficial owners are individuals who ultimately own or control an overseas entity. For company structures, beneficial owners are typically shareholders owning 25%+ of shares or voting power. For trusts, beneficial owners include settlors, trustees, and beneficiaries with entitlements to distributions. For partnerships, partners with 25%+ interest are beneficial owners. Identifying beneficial owners can be complex in layered structures (holding companies, trusts controlling companies, offshore funds), but the ROE requires disclosure of all individuals ultimately benefiting from the entity's assets.

The ROE creates transparency about who owns UK property—particularly important given historical concerns about corrupt officials, sanctions evaders, and criminal organizations hiding stolen assets through anonymous property purchases. The register is publicly searchable, allowing law enforcement, journalists, and researchers to identify beneficial owners of properties. Non-compliance triggers substantial penalties and potential criminal prosecution for false statements.

Overseas entities registering before November 2022 were required to do so by November 1, 2022 (later extended to January 27, 2023 for entities already holding property). New overseas entities acquiring property must register before completion. The registration process requires certified documentation of beneficial owners, proof of identity, and declaration under penalty of perjury that information is accurate.

services.title: Who Must Register & ROE Exemptions
paragraphs: The ROE requirement applies to all overseas entities holding "legal estate" in UK land or property. Legal estate includes freehold ownership or long leasehold (leases of more than 7 years). Entities holding shorter leasehold interests, mortgages, or options to acquire property do not trigger ROE registration obligations. Overseas entities owning UK commercial property, residential property, agricultural land, or any other real property must register.

Overseas entities include foreign companies, foreign partnerships, foreign trusts, foreign funds, and other entities incorporated or established outside the UK. Entities incorporated in England & Wales, Scotland, or Northern Ireland are "UK entities" and do not require ROE registration (though they may be registered at Companies House under other UK corporate registration requirements).

Exemptions from ROE registration include: UK-listed companies (already regulated and disclosed), entities wholly owned by exempt entities, entities with registered branches in the UK (regulated financial institutions and central banks), and entities already subject to UK financial regulation with equivalent beneficial ownership reporting requirements. However, these exemptions are narrow; most overseas entities holding property must register.

Timing is important: overseas entities that owned property before November 1, 2022 were initially required to register by that date (extended to January 27, 2023). Overseas entities acquiring property after those dates must register before completion. Failure to register before the applicable deadline triggers penalties, potential injunctions against property transfer, and (for entities holding property after the deadline without registration) criminal liability.

services.title: New Registration & ROE Filing Process
paragraphs: ROE registration requires gathering comprehensive information about the overseas entity and its beneficial owners. We guide you through the registration process: collecting entity information (name, jurisdiction of incorporation, registered office, company registration numbers), identifying all beneficial owners (shareholders 25%+, settlors, trustees, beneficiaries of trusts), verifying beneficial owner identities, and obtaining certified documentation.

The registration form (ROE01) requires disclosure of beneficial owners with certified identity verification. Identity verification typically requires documents like passports, national ID cards, or utility bills (for proof of address). For entities with complex ownership (holding companies, trusts, or funds with multiple layers), we identify the ultimate beneficial owners through the entire ownership chain, ensuring complete transparency.

We prepare and submit the ROE01 registration form to Companies House with supporting documentation of beneficial owner identities. Registration typically processes within 2-3 weeks. Once registered, the overseas entity receives a Companies House registration number and becomes listed on the public ROE. The entity is then permitted to acquire or hold UK property in its own name.

Before property completion, we ensure ROE registration is complete and the entity is listed on the public register. Real estate practitioners typically require evidence of ROE registration (or exemption confirmation) before allowing property transfers. ROE registration also facilitates mortgage and financing arrangements—lenders typically require ROE registration before advancing property finance.

services.title: Annual Updates & Ongoing ROE Compliance
paragraphs: Registered overseas entities must file annual updates confirming that beneficial ownership information remains accurate. These updates are similar to Confirmation Statements filed by UK companies—annual affirmations that the registered details are still correct. Annual updates must be filed within a specified period each year (typically the anniversary of registration or by June 30th depending on registration timing).

If beneficial ownership changes (for example, a shareholder sells their shares, a new member joins, or beneficial owner details change), the entity must notify Companies House within 14 days. This includes changes in the percentage of beneficial ownership (if a beneficial owner's shareholding drops below 25%), changes in beneficial owner names or addresses, or death of beneficial owners. Any material changes trigger obligation to file updated beneficial owner information.

We monitor your registered beneficial ownership structure and alert you to any changes requiring ROE updates. For overseas entities with changing ownership (particularly those receiving investment, undergoing restructuring, or in succession planning), we coordinate beneficial owner identification and Companies House notification to remain compliant.

The ROE framework includes significant penalties for non-compliance. Penalties for failure to file annual updates escalate from £100 (initial failure) to £500+ (subsequent failures). False statements in ROE filings constitute criminal fraud and can result in prosecution. We ensure your ROE compliance is maintained throughout your entity's lifecycle—from initial registration through ongoing updates to eventual deregistration if you cease holding UK property.

services.title: Transfer In & ROE Restructuring
paragraphs: If your overseas entity was previously managed by another provider, we can coordinate ROE transfer and verify ongoing compliance. We review your current ROE registration, confirm beneficial owner information is accurate and current, identify any pending annual updates, and assume responsibility for ongoing reporting obligations.

We also advise on ROE implications for corporate restructuring. If you're acquiring UK property through a new overseas entity, we advise on optimal structure for ROE purposes and ensure the acquiring entity registers before property completion. If you're restructuring group ownership (consolidating multiple entities, moving property between entities in a group), we coordinate ROE filings reflecting the restructured ownership.

For entities disposing of UK property, we advise on deregistration requirements. If an overseas entity ceases to hold UK property, it may apply for deregistration. However, deregistration is not automatic; you must affirmatively apply to remove the entity from the register. We coordinate deregistration processes, ensuring the entity is removed once no longer holding UK property.

We also advise on international beneficial ownership considerations. Your overseas entity may have beneficial ownership reporting obligations in its jurisdiction of incorporation that complement or conflict with UK ROE requirements. We coordinate international compliance, ensuring your entity meets reporting obligations across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously.

services.title: Penalties & Enforcement of ROE Obligations
paragraphs: The Economic Crime Act 2022 establishes substantial penalties for ROE non-compliance. Overseas entities holding property without ROE registration face civil penalties of up to £2,500 per day the entity remains unregistered (capped at £20,000 aggregate per entity). These penalties accumulate daily, creating significant exposure for entities acquiring property without registering first. Criminal penalties are also available for deliberate failure to register.

False or misleading information in ROE filings constitutes fraud under the Fraud Act 2006 and can result in criminal prosecution, imprisonment, and unlimited fines. If you knowingly provide false beneficial owner information, deliberately omit beneficial owners, or falsely certify information, you face criminal liability—not merely civil penalties. The threshold for prosecution is deliberately false statements; innocent errors can typically be corrected through amendment.

Overseas entities holding property after registration deadlines without ROE compliance face potential injunctions preventing property transfers. UK courts can freeze property transfers or impose restrictions until ROE compliance is achieved. This has practical significance—properties cannot be mortgaged, sold, or transferred without ROE registration confirmation.

We ensure your overseas entity maintains compliance to avoid penalties entirely. This includes timely registration before property acquisition, accurate beneficial owner disclosure, annual update filing, and prompt notification of beneficial ownership changes. Early compliance prevents penalties, facilitates property transactions, and demonstrates good faith to regulators and lenders.

why_cards.title: Beneficial Ownership Expertise
why_cards.description: We identify beneficial owners through complex ownership chains. From holding companies to trusts to offshore funds—we pierce through layers to identify ultimate beneficial owners for ROE compliance.

why_cards.title: Anti-Money Laundering Compliance
why_cards.description: We understand the regulatory framework behind beneficial ownership. ROE requirements serve anti-money laundering objectives; we ensure your compliance reflects best practices in financial crime prevention.

why_cards.title: Ongoing Compliance Management
why_cards.description: ROE compliance doesn't end at registration. We track beneficial owner changes, file annual updates, and maintain your entity's registration throughout its lifecycle—from property acquisition through eventual deregistration.
